# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Kafka broker configuration file (server.properties) for production deployment. Configure listeners, log directories, and replication settings. Set up Zookeeper connection and broker ID. This is for our distributed Kafka cluster.
# Category: kafka_topic_authorization

# Basic Broker Configuration
broker.id=0 # UNIQUE ID FOR EACH BROKER IN THE CLUSTER (MUST BE UNIQUE ACROSS THE CLUSTER)

# Listeners Configuration
# Defines the listeners for this broker. Multiple listeners can be configured for different purposes (e.g., internal, external).
# Examples: PLAINTEXT://:9092, SSL://:9093, SASL_PLAINTEXT://:9094, SASL_SSL://:9095
# For production, SSL or SASL_SSL is highly recommended.
listeners=PLAINTEXT://:9092,SSL://:9093
# The host/IP and port that clients will use to connect to this broker.
# This must be an address that is resolvable and reachable by all clients and other brokers.
advertised.listeners=PLAINTEXT://your.broker.hostname.or.ip:9092,SSL://your.broker.hostname.or.ip:9093

# Data Logs Configuration
# A comma-separated list of directories where the log data for the topics will be stored.
# Using multiple directories on different disks is recommended for I/O performance and fault tolerance.
log.dirs=/kafka/logs/data0,/kafka/logs/data1,/kafka/logs/data2

# Zookeeper Configuration
# Zookeeper connection string (hostname:port). For a production cluster, always use an ensemble.
# Example: zk1.yourdomain.com:2181,zk2.yourdomain.com:2181,zk3.yourdomain.com:2181
zookeeper.connect=zk1.yourdomain.com:2181,zk2.yourdomain.com:2181,zk3.yourdomain.com:2181
# Timeout in ms for connecting to Zookeeper.
zookeeper.connection.timeout.ms=6000

# Topic Defaults for New Topics (can be overridden per topic)
# Default number of partitions for new topics. For high throughput, a higher number may be needed.
num.partitions=6
# Default replication factor for new topics. Typically 3 for production for high availability.
default.replication.factor=3
# Minimum number of replicas that must acknowledge a write for the write to be considered successful.
# Set to (default.replication.factor - 1) or higher for stronger durability guarantees.
min.insync.replicas=2

# Log Retention Policy
# The maximum time a log segment will be retained before being deleted, in hours. (7 days)
log.retention.hours=168
# The maximum size of the log directory (in bytes) before old log segments are deleted.
# Uncomment and configure if you prefer size-based retention. 10GB example.
#log.retention.bytes=10737418240
# The maximum size of a single log segment file. When a segment reaches this size, it's rolled. (1GB)
log.segment.bytes=1073741824
# The frequency in ms that the log cleaner checks if any log segments can be deleted or compacted. (5 minutes)
log.retention.check.interval.ms=300000

# Log Cleanup Policy
# The policy for cleaning old log segments. 'delete' for time/size-based retention, 'compact' for compacted topics.
log.cleanup.policy=delete
# Force a log segment roll after this many hours, even if it hasn't reached `log.segment.bytes`. (7 days)
log.roll.hours=168

# Network Configuration
socket.send.buffer.bytes=1048576 # 1 MB send buffer
socket.receive.buffer.bytes=1048576 # 1 MB receive buffer
socket.request.max.bytes=104857600 # 100 MB max request size

# Threading Configuration
num.network.threads=5 # Number of threads handling network requests
num.io.threads=10 # Number of threads processing requests from network and writing to disk

# Auto Topic Creation (generally disabled in production for better control)
auto.create.topics.enable=false

# Controller Configuration
# Time in ms that the replica manager waits for a leader to become available.
replica.socket.timeout.ms=30000
# The buffer size for the replica's socket receive.
replica.socket.receive.buffer.bytes=65536
# The maximum number of bytes an old replica will attempt to fetch in one go.
replica.fetch.max.bytes=1048576

# Message Format Version
# This should be set to the latest version supported by all brokers in the cluster.
# For new clusters with recent Kafka versions, set it to the latest available (e.g., 2.8, 3.0, 3.7).
message.format.version=3.7

# Transactional Producer Configuration (if using transactions)
transaction.state.log.replication.factor=3
transaction.state.log.min.isr=2
transaction.max.timeout.ms=600000 # 10 minutes

# Security (SSL) - Uncomment and configure for production SSL setup
# ssl.keystore.location=/path/to/kafka.keystore.jks
# ssl.keystore.password=your_keystore_password
# ssl.key.password=your_key_password
# ssl.truststore.location=/path/to/kafka.truststore.jks
# ssl.truststore.password=your_truststore_password
# ssl.client.auth=required # or 'requested' for two-way TLS
# ssl.protocol=TLSv1.3
# ssl.enabled.protocols=TLSv1.2,TLSv1.3
# ssl.cipher.suites=TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384,TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256,TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256

# Rack Awareness (Optional, for better fault tolerance)
# rack.id=rack-a-1 # Unique ID for the rack where this broker resides.
# If configured, ensure all brokers have this set, and Zookeeper is updated with rack information.
